steps.title: Find the lower bound
steps.content: The expression $2^x$ is always positive, but it can get arbitrarily close to $0$ as $x$ becomes very negative.

steps.title: Apply that to g
steps.content: For $$g(x)=\frac{1}{13}(6\cdot2^x+5)$$ the smallest value approached is $$\frac{1}{13}(6\cdot0+5)=\frac{5}{13}$$ Because $2^x$ is never actually $0$, $g(x)$ is always greater than $\frac{5}{13}$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The greatest possible value of $k$ is $\frac{5}{13}$, so B is correct.
